text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCOUNT NUMBER: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED], MD — Gynecologic Oncology
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
SERVICE: Gynecologic Pathology

CLINICAL HISTORY: 47-year-old female with known high-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC) of the bilateral ovaries and fallopian tubes, FIGO stage IIIC, presenting for primary debulking surgery. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC; 5382insC) identified on preoperative germline testing. Preoperative CA-125: 3,847 U/mL. Prior diagnostic laparoscopy and omental biopsy confirmed HGSC with WT-1 and PAX8 positivity, p53 aberrant pattern, Ki-67 78%. Staging CT demonstrated bilateral adnexal masses (right 7.2 cm, left 5.8 cm), omental caking, peritoneal carcinomatosis, and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy (PCI estimated 18). Procedure: exploratory laparotomy, total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (TAH-BSO), infracolic omentectomy, appendectomy, bilateral pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection, resection of peritoneal implants from the right paracolic gutter, sigmoid mesentery, and left pelvic sidewall, and right hemidiaphragm peritoneal stripping.

---

SPECIMEN DESIGNATIONS AND FINAL DIAGNOSES

SPECIMEN A: RIGHT OVARY AND FALLOPIAN TUBE (received in formalin, labeled "right adnexa")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN A:
  RIGHT OVARY: HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
    - Extensive involvement of ovarian parenchyma and ovarian surface
    - Lymphovascular invasion identified
    - Tumor size: 7.2 cm in greatest dimension
  RIGHT FALLOPIAN TUBE: SEROUS TUBAL INTRAEPITHELIAL CARCINOMA (STIC) OF THE FIMBRIATED END WITH INVASIVE CARCINOMA INVOLVING THE TUBAL WALL
    - STIC identified at fimbriated end based on p53 aberrant staining and increased Ki-67
    - Invasive high-grade serous carcinoma extending into muscularis; tubal serosa involved
    - Findings support fallopian tube as the primary site of origin

SPECIMEN B: LEFT OVARY AND FALLOPIAN TUBE (received in formalin, labeled "left adnexa")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN B:
  LEFT OVARY: HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
    - Involvement of ovarian parenchyma with surface implantation
    - No lymphovascular invasion identified in sections examined
    - Tumor size: 5.8 cm in greatest dimension
  LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE: SEROUS TUBAL INTRAEPITHELIAL CARCINOMA (STIC) OF THE FIMBRIATED END, WITHOUT INVASIVE CARCINOMA
    - p53 aberrant staining and markedly elevated Ki-67 consistent with STIC
    - No invasion of tubal muscularis or serosa identified

SPECIMEN C: UTERUS WITH CERVIX (received in formalin, labeled "uterus")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN C:
  UTERUS: NO EVIDENCE OF MALIGNANCY
    - Endometrium: atrophic; no hyperplasia, no carcinoma, no involvement by serous carcinoma
    - Myometrium: unremarkable; no myometrial invasion
    - Cervix: ectocervix and endocervical canal with no dysplasia or malignancy
    - Serosa: no tumor implants identified
    - Uterine weight: 72 grams

SPECIMEN D: INFRACOLIC OMENTUM (received in formalin, labeled "omentum")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN D:
  OMENTUM: METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
    - Extensive replacement of omental adipose tissue by metastatic carcinoma (omental cake)
    - Multiple confluent nodules and sheets of tumor, largest deposit 6.4 cm
    - Lymphovascular invasion identified within omental tissue
    - No normal residual omental architecture identified in involved areas

SPECIMEN E: APPENDIX (received in formalin, labeled "appendix")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN E:
  APPENDIX: METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA INVOLVING APPENDICEAL SEROSA
    - One peritoneal implant on the appendiceal serosal surface, measuring 0.8 cm
    - No invasion of the muscularis propria or mucosa
    - Appendiceal mucosa: unremarkable; no appendiceal primary neoplasm identified
    - No low-grade appendiceal mucinous neoplasm (LAMN)

SPECIMEN F: RIGHT PARACOLIC GUTTER PERITONEUM (received in formalin in separate containers, labeled "right paracolic gutter peritoneal implants")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN F:
  RIGHT PARACOLIC GUTTER PERITONEUM: THREE PERITONEAL IMPLANTS POSITIVE FOR METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
    - Implant F1: 2.1 cm — invasive implant with stromal desmoplasia
    - Implant F2: 1.3 cm — invasive implant
    - Implant F3: 0.6 cm — desmoplastic implant
    - All implants show invasive features (not non-invasive/exophytic type)

SPECIMEN G: LEFT PELVIC SIDEWALL PERITONEUM (received in formalin, labeled "left pelvic sidewall implants")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN G:
  LEFT PELVIC SIDEWALL PERITONEUM: TWO PERITONEAL IMPLANTS POSITIVE FOR METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
    - Implant G1: 1.4 cm — invasive implant
    - Implant G2: 0.9 cm — invasive implant

SPECIMEN H: RIGHT HEMIDIAPHRAGM PERITONEUM (received in formalin, labeled "right hemidiaphragm peritoneum")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN H:
  RIGHT HEMIDIAPHRAGM PERITONEUM: ONE PERITONEAL IMPLANT POSITIVE FOR METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
    - Single implant measuring 1.5 cm on the peritoneal surface of the right hemidiaphragm
    - Invasive into peritoneal fibrous tissue; no invasion of diaphragmatic muscle identified

SPECIMEN I: BILATERAL PELVIC LYMPH NODES (received in formalin, multiple labeled containers)

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN I:
  BILATERAL PELVIC LYMPH NODES: 4 OF 18 LYMPH NODES POSITIVE FOR METASTATIC CARCINOMA
    - Right external iliac: 2 of 6 nodes positive (largest positive node 1.4 cm; extranodal extension absent)
    - Left external iliac: 0 of 3 nodes positive
    - Right obturator: 1 of 4 nodes positive (0.7 cm deposit; no extranodal extension)
    - Left obturator: 1 of 5 nodes positive (0.9 cm deposit; no extranodal extension)
    Total pelvic nodes examined: 18; positive: 4

SPECIMEN J: BILATERAL PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES (received in formalin, labeled "para-aortic lymph nodes")

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN J:
  PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES: 1 OF 9 LYMPH NODES POSITIVE FOR METASTATIC CARCINOMA
    - Right para-aortic: 1 of 5 nodes positive (1.1 cm deposit; focal extranodal extension present)
    - Left para-aortic: 0 of 4 nodes positive
    Total para-aortic nodes examined: 9; positive: 1

SPECIMEN K: ASCITES CYTOLOGY (2,800 mL ascitic fluid collected intraoperatively, submitted in CytoLyt)

FINAL DIAGNOSIS — SPECIMEN K:
  ASCITIC FLUID (PERITONEAL WASHING): POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT EPITHELIAL CELLS
    - Papillary clusters and three-dimensional aggregates of highly atypical epithelial cells
    - Cytomorphology consistent with high-grade serous carcinoma
    - Psammomatous calcifications identified on cell block preparation
    - Cell block immunostaining: WT-1 positive (diffuse nuclear), PAX8 positive (diffuse nuclear)

---

PATHOLOGIC STAGE (AJCC 8th edition / FIGO 2014):
  pT3c pN1 M0
  FIGO Stage IIIC

SURGICAL MARGINS:
  All surgical margins negative for carcinoma (R0 resection)
  Parametrial margins: negative
  Vaginal cuff margin: negative
  Omental resection margin: uninvolved

---

GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION

SPECIMEN A — RIGHT OVARY AND FALLOPIAN TUBE:
Received fresh then placed in formalin. The right adnexa consists of a lobulated ovarian mass measuring 7.2 x 6.0 x 5.5 cm and an attached fallopian tube measuring 9.5 cm in length x 0.8 cm in diameter. The external surface of the ovarian mass is bosselated and focally ruptured with tan-gray, firm tumor deposits studding the surface. On serial sectioning, the ovarian parenchyma is almost entirely replaced by gray-white to yellow solid tumor with focal areas of central necrosis and hemorrhage. Residual ovarian stroma is not appreciated. The cut surface has a heterogeneous solid and focally cystic appearance; the cystic component is largely obliterated. The fallopian tube is grossly distorted with a bulging fimbriated end; the fimbria are replaced by firm gray-white tissue measuring up to 0.8 cm. Serial sectioning of the tube reveals gray-white tumor infiltrating the muscularis at the fimbriated end. The proximal tube is unremarkable. Representative sections submitted in 12 cassettes (A1–A12).

SPECIMEN B — LEFT OVARY AND FALLOPIAN TUBE:
Received in formalin. The left adnexa consists of a lobulated ovarian mass measuring 5.8 x 4.3 x 4.0 cm and an attached fallopian tube measuring 8.2 cm in length x 0.7 cm in diameter. The external surface of the ovarian mass is smooth over approximately 70% of its circumference, with a 2.5 x 1.8 cm area of roughened, gray-white surface nodularity representing serosal implantation. On serial sectioning, the ovarian parenchyma is replaced by gray-white solid tumor with foci of hemorrhage; a small residual cystic cavity (approximately 1.2 cm) with thin walls and clear fluid is identified. The fallopian tube fimbria are mildly thickened; serial sectioning reveals subtle gray-white thickening of the tubal mucosa at the fimbriated end without grossly visible mass lesion. The proximal tube is unremarkable. Representative sections submitted in 8 cassettes (B1–B8).

SPECIMEN C — UTERUS WITH CERVIX:
Received in formalin. The uterus with attached cervix weighs 72 grams and measures 7.8 x 4.2 x 3.1 cm. The external serosal surface is smooth and glistening without implants. The cervix is smooth with a pinpoint os. On bivalving, the endometrial cavity is narrow with an endometrial thickness of less than 1 mm (atrophic). The myometrium is homogeneous tan-white and measures up to 1.8 cm in thickness; no masses, fibroids, or areas of adenomyosis identified. Sections submitted in 6 cassettes (C1–C6) including full-thickness myometrium, anterior and posterior cervix, lower uterine segment, and parametrial tissue.

SPECIMEN D — INFRACOLIC OMENTUM:
Received in formalin. The omentum is received as a large, irregularly shaped aggregate of fibroadipose tissue measuring 22 x 15 x 3.5 cm and weighing 148 grams. The tissue is extensively replaced by confluent solid gray-white and tan-yellow firm tumor (omental cake). The cut surface shows near-complete replacement of the omental fat by tumor in a nodular, coalescent pattern; focal central necrosis (up to 20% of involved areas) and chalky-white calcifications (psammoma body deposits) are noted. The largest discrete tumor deposit measures 6.4 cm. Residual omental fat is identified in a small area (approximately 2 x 3 cm) at one margin, without tumor involvement. Representative sections submitted in 8 cassettes (D1–D8).

SPECIMEN E — APPENDIX:
Received in formalin. The appendix measures 5.8 cm in length x 0.8 cm in diameter. The serosal surface has a smooth tan appearance over most of its circumference; at the mid-appendix, there is a firm, gray-white serosal nodule measuring 0.8 x 0.6 cm. On cross-sectioning, the appendiceal lumen is patent and the mucosa is tan-pink without ulceration or masses. The wall thickness is normal. Representative sections submitted in 3 cassettes (E1–E3) including the serosal nodule, proximal, mid, and distal appendix, and appendiceal tip.

SPECIMEN F — RIGHT PARACOLIC GUTTER PERITONEAL IMPLANTS:
Received in formalin in three separately labeled containers. Container F1: an irregular fragment of fibroadipose tissue and peritoneum measuring 3.2 x 2.5 x 1.8 cm with a firm gray-white nodular lesion measuring 2.1 x 1.9 x 1.4 cm involving one surface. Container F2: a fragment of peritoneum measuring 2.0 x 1.5 x 0.8 cm with a 1.3 cm gray-white firm nodule. Container F3: a fragment of fibrous peritoneum measuring 1.0 x 0.7 x 0.4 cm with a 0.6 cm tan-firm implant. All submitted in 3 cassettes (F1–F3).

SPECIMEN G — LEFT PELVIC SIDEWALL PERITONEAL IMPLANTS:
Received in formalin in two separately labeled containers. Container G1: a fragment of fibroconnective tissue and peritoneum measuring 2.5 x 2.0 x 1.0 cm with a 1.4 x 1.1 cm firm gray-white nodule. Container G2: a fragment of peritoneum measuring 1.5 x 1.2 x 0.5 cm with a 0.9 cm firm gray-white implant. Submitted in 2 cassettes (G1–G2).

SPECIMEN H — RIGHT HEMIDIAPHRAGM PERITONEUM:
Received in formalin. A fragment of glistening fibrous peritoneal tissue measuring 2.5 x 2.0 x 0.4 cm with a firm, white-gray nodule measuring 1.5 x 1.2 x 0.4 cm on the peritoneal surface. The deep aspect shows fibrous tissue without muscle. Submitted entirely in 1 cassette (H1).

SPECIMEN I — BILATERAL PELVIC LYMPH NODES:
Received in formalin in multiple labeled containers representing: right external iliac (6 nodes identified, ranging from 0.4 to 1.6 cm; largest two are firm and tan-white with loss of normal architecture), left external iliac (3 nodes, 0.3–0.8 cm), right obturator (4 nodes, 0.4–0.9 cm; one firm tan-white node 0.9 cm), and left obturator (5 nodes, 0.3–1.1 cm; one firm tan-white 1.1 cm node). Total of 18 lymph nodes submitted in 10 cassettes (I1–I10), entirely submitted for smaller nodes, bisected for larger nodes.

SPECIMEN J — BILATERAL PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES:
Received in formalin in two labeled containers. Right para-aortic: 5 nodes identified ranging from 0.5 to 1.4 cm; the largest node (1.4 cm) is firm and pale tan on cut surface, largely replaced by tumor with focal extranodal extension into perinodal fat visible grossly. Left para-aortic: 4 nodes, 0.3–0.7 cm, grossly unremarkable. Total 9 nodes submitted in 5 cassettes (J1–J5).

SPECIMEN K — ASCITES/PERITONEAL WASHINGS:
Received 2,800 mL of straw-colored to slightly turbid peritoneal fluid in multiple specimen containers. Fluid submitted for cytologic evaluation with ThinPrep preparation and cell block preparation. Cell block constructed from centrifuged pellet embedded in paraffin.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

SPECIMENS A AND B (Right and Left Ovaries / Fallopian Tubes):
Sections of the right ovary show extensive replacement of the ovarian parenchyma by a high-grade epithelial neoplasm arranged in solid sheets, papillary structures, glandular configurations, and slit-like spaces. The tumor cells are highly pleomorphic with large, vesicular nuclei, prominent macronucleoli, and markedly irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are numerous, including atypical tripolar forms. Geographic areas of tumor necrosis and hyalinization are identified. Calcospherites (psammoma bodies) are scattered throughout. Stromal desmoplasia is prominent. Lymphovascular invasion is unequivocally identified in multiple sections. The ovarian surface is involved by carcinoma with focal surface disruption.

Sections of the right fallopian tube fimbria show replacement of the normal tubal epithelium at the fimbriated end by highly atypical epithelial cells with markedly abnormal p53 staining (see ancillary studies) and increased mitotic activity — morphologically and immunohistochemically consistent with serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC). Additionally, frankly invasive high-grade serous carcinoma is identified infiltrating the muscularis of the fimbriated end with involvement of the tubal serosa. The SEE-FIM (Sectioning and Extensively Examining the Fimbria) protocol was followed. Findings strongly support the fallopian tube, specifically the fimbriated end, as the site of primary tumor origin, consistent with current paradigms of high-grade serous carcinogenesis.

Sections of the left ovary show high-grade serous carcinoma involving the ovarian parenchyma and ovarian surface (serosal implantation), morphologically identical to the right ovarian tumor. No lymphovascular invasion is identified in sections examined. Sections of the left fallopian tube fimbria show STIC (aberrant p53 staining, elevated Ki-67, cytologic atypia) without invasion of the tubal wall, consistent with in-situ precursor lesion.

SPECIMEN C (Uterus):
Sections of the endometrium show an atrophic pattern with inactive glands and scant stroma. No endometrial carcinoma, no complex hyperplasia, and no surface involvement by serous carcinoma. The myometrium is benign. Cervix shows unremarkable ectocervical squamous and endocervical glandular mucosa without dysplasia, carcinoma, or involvement by serous carcinoma. Serosal surfaces are free of tumor.

SPECIMEN D (Omentum):
Sections show extensive replacement of omental adipose tissue and fibrous stroma by metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma. The tumor forms solid sheets, papillary clusters, glandular nests, and slit-like spaces, with abundant psammoma bodies and geographic necrosis. Lymphovascular channels within the omentum contain tumor emboli. Residual omental fat is identified at the periphery of some sections.

SPECIMEN E (Appendix):
Sections show a peritoneal implant on the appendiceal serosal surface composed of nests of high-grade serous carcinoma with desmoplastic stroma and psammomatous calcification. The implant does not invade the muscularis propria or mucosa. The appendiceal mucosa is unremarkable with no evidence of goblet cell carcinoid, low-grade appendiceal mucinous neoplasm, or other primary appendiceal pathology.

SPECIMENS F, G, H (Peritoneal Implants — right paracolic gutter, left pelvic sidewall, right hemidiaphragm):
All peritoneal implants demonstrate invasive high-grade serous carcinoma with similar morphology to the primary tumor: sheets and papillary aggregates of highly pleomorphic epithelial cells, prominent nucleoli, numerous mitotic figures, psammoma bodies, and desmoplastic stromal reaction. The implants are classified as invasive type (not non-invasive/desmoplastic exophytic type). There is infiltration of the peritoneal fibrous tissue in all specimens. No involvement of underlying musculoaponeurotic tissue is identified in specimens F or G. In specimen H (right hemidiaphragm), the tumor infiltrates the peritoneal fibrous tissue but does not invade diaphragmatic skeletal muscle.

SPECIMEN I (Pelvic Lymph Nodes):
Sections of pelvic lymph nodes demonstrate metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 4 of 18 nodes as noted above. Involved nodes show partial to near-complete replacement of the nodal parenchyma by tumor with residual subcapsular sinuses in some areas. No extranodal extension is identified in pelvic nodes. Uninvolved nodes show reactive follicular hyperplasia.

SPECIMEN J (Para-aortic Lymph Nodes):
Sections of right para-aortic lymph node (largest, 1.4 cm) show near-complete replacement by metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma with focal extranodal extension into perinodal adipose tissue, spanning approximately 1.2 mm. Remaining para-aortic nodes are free of metastatic carcinoma.

SPECIMEN K (Ascites Cytology / Peritoneal Washings):
Cytospin and ThinPrep preparations show numerous papillary clusters, three-dimensional ball-like aggregates, and isolated malignant cells with markedly enlarged nuclei, prominent nucleoli, irregular nuclear membranes, and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios. Psammomatous calcifications are identified. Background shows inflammatory cells and mesothelial cells. Cell block sections confirm malignant epithelial cells positive for WT-1 and PAX8 (see ancillary studies). Consistent with high-grade serous carcinoma.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on representative sections from Specimen A — right ovary; results applied to all anatomically concordant specimens unless otherwise noted):

  WT-1 (clone 6F-H2):          POSITIVE — diffuse strong nuclear staining (3+)
  PAX8 (polyclonal):            POSITIVE — diffuse strong nuclear staining (3+)
  p53 (clone DO-7):             ABERRANT PATTERN — diffuse strong nuclear overexpression (>80% of tumor nuclei, 3+), consistent with TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation; this pattern excludes TP53 null/complete loss pattern
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30):     POSITIVE — diffuse (3+)
  CK20 (clone Ks20.8):         NEGATIVE
  CA-125 (clone M11):           POSITIVE — diffuse (2-3+)
  ER (clone SP1):               WEAKLY POSITIVE — 25-30% of nuclei, weak intensity (1+)
  PR (clone 1E2):               NEGATIVE
  Napsin A:                     NEGATIVE
  CDX2:                         NEGATIVE
  SATB2:                        NEGATIVE
  p16 (clone E6H4):             DIFFUSE BLOCK POSITIVE — diffuse strong nuclear and cytoplasmic positivity (consistent with high-grade serous morphology; not indicative of HPV)

  Ki-67 (clone MIB-1):         PROLIFERATION INDEX APPROXIMATELY 75-80%

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY — RIGHT FALLOPIAN TUBE (STIC CONFIRMATION):
  p53 (clone DO-7):             ABERRANT — diffuse strong nuclear overexpression in the atypical tubal epithelium at fimbriated end; abrupt transition to wild-type staining in the adjacent normal epithelium
  Ki-67 (clone MIB-1):         Markedly elevated in atypical tubal epithelium (estimated 40-50% in STIC region vs. <5% in adjacent normal epithelium)
  IHC profile confirms STIC diagnosis at fimbriated end of right fallopian tube.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY — ASCITES CELL BLOCK (Specimen K):
  WT-1:                         POSITIVE — diffuse nuclear
  PAX8:                         POSITIVE — diffuse nuclear
  Calretinin:                   NEGATIVE (excludes reactive mesothelium as dominant population)
  MOC31 (EpCAM):                POSITIVE — confirms epithelial nature

ADDITIONAL ANCILLARY STUDIES — STATUS PENDING AT TIME OF FINAL SIGN-OUT:
  Mismatch repair (MMR) immunohistochemistry (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2) — ordered per institutional protocol for all gynecologic malignancies; result pending at time of report issuance. Addendum to follow.
  BRCA1 protein expression by IHC — ordered for research correlation; result pending. Addendum to follow.

---

COMMENT:

The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings in all specimens are consistent with high-grade serous carcinoma of Mullerian origin (HGSC). The overall pattern is fully concordant with the prior diagnostic omental biopsy. Importantly, serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC) with co-existing invasive carcinoma at the fimbriated end of the right fallopian tube (with the SEE-FIM protocol performed) supports the current evidence-based paradigm that many apparent "ovarian" HGSCs originate in the fallopian tube, specifically the secretory cells of the fimbriated end. The bilateral ovarian involvement, bilateral STIC, and peritoneal spread are consistent with fallopian tube origin with secondary spread to the ovaries and peritoneum.

The p53 aberrant staining pattern (diffuse strong overexpression, gain-of-function type) is characteristic of HGSC and correlates with the somatic TP53 p.R248W pathogenic variant identified on preoperative tumor NGS. No mucinous, endometrioid, clear cell, or low-grade serous features are identified. Endometrioid and clear cell carcinoma have been excluded by morphology and IHC.

Total lymph nodes examined: 27 (18 pelvic + 9 para-aortic). Lymph node positivity: 5 of 27 (18.5%). Focal extranodal extension noted in the right para-aortic station only.

All surgical resection margins are negative for carcinoma. In the context of the operative report documenting no gross residual disease at conclusion of surgery, the pathologic findings confirm an R0 (complete gross) resection.

Pathologic staging per AJCC 8th Edition:
  Primary tumor: pT3c (peritoneal metastasis beyond pelvis, one or more with maximum dimension >2 cm, and/or retroperitoneal or inguinal lymph node involvement)
  Regional lymph nodes: pN1 (regional lymph node metastasis)
  Distant metastasis: pM0 (no distant metastasis)
  Overall: pT3c pN1 M0 — FIGO Stage IIIC

---

SIGNED:
[Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Division of Gynecologic Pathology

ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED

DICTATED AND REVIEWED BY: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
TRANSCRIBED BY: [Pathology Resident, MD / Pathology Assistant]

CC: Gynecologic Oncology Service; Medical Oncology

*** ADDENDUM PENDING: MMR IHC results; BRCA1 IHC. Addendum will be issued upon availability. ***
